title: Chronos: A Physics-Informed Full-History Framework for Non-Markovian Long-Horizon Manipulation
title_zh: Chronos：一种物理信息驱动的全历史框架，用于非马尔可夫长期操控
abstract: General-purpose robot policies should be modeled as dynamical systems, yet many VLA and generative imitation policies still rely on present observations or short windows. This Markovian shortcut fails in memory-dependent manipulation: identical observations can demand different actions after different histories. We present Chronos, a physics-informed full-history framework for non-Markovian long-horizon manipulation. The key idea is to elevate observation history from auxiliary context to the latent state of the policy dynamics. At each physical control step, Chronos forms one state-representative token by fusing observation and proprioception, so the token sequence is aligned one-to-one with physical time. A selective state space model propagates this causal historical state, which conditions a multimodal coarse action prior through implicit maximum likelihood estimation (IMLE). This prior is then refined by a second-order Schrodinger-inspired bridge that predicts acceleration fields, yielding smoother and more physically grounded robot motion. Across 16 simulated tasks and 4 real-world experiments, Chronos is evaluated on precision insertion, general manipulation, and memory-dependent long-horizon control. On RMBench, where success requires remembering task phase, Chronos achieves 73.6% average success, outperforming Markovian VLA baseline pi0.5 by +62.4 percentage points, a 6.6x relative gain, while using 10x fewer parameters. It also surpasses the memory VLA Mem-0 by 22.8 points while using over 30x fewer parameters. In real-world dual-arm experiments using a single RGB camera, Chronos achieves 78% average success over four tasks, including 72% on the three memory-dependent tasks, whereas pi0.5 achieves 7% overall and 0% on the memory-dependent subset. These results suggest that history should not be treated as auxiliary context, but as the latent state of the manipulation policy.
abstract_zh: 通用机器人策略应建模为动力系统，但许多VLA和生成式模仿策略仍依赖当前观测或短时间窗口。这种马尔可夫捷径在依赖记忆的操控中失效：相同观测在不同历史后可能要求不同动作。我们提出Chronos，一种物理信息驱动的全历史框架，用于非马尔可夫长期操控。关键思想是将观测历史从辅助上下文提升为策略动力学的潜在状态。在每个物理控制步，Chronos通过融合观测和本体感知形成一个状态代表令牌，使令牌序列与物理时间一一对齐。选择性状态空间模型传播这个因果历史状态，通过隐式最大似然估计（IMLE）条件化一个多模态粗动作先验。该先验随后由二阶薛定谔启发桥细化，预测加速度场，产生更平滑、更物理合理的机器人运动。在16个模拟任务和4个真实世界实验中，Chronos在精密插入、通用操控和依赖记忆的长期控制上进行了评估。在RMBench上，成功需要记住任务阶段，Chronos达到73.6%平均成功率，超过马尔可夫VLA基线pi0.5达62.4个百分点（相对提升6.6倍），同时使用少10倍的参数。它还超过记忆VLA Mem-0达22.8个百分点，同时使用少30倍以上的参数。在真实世界双臂实验中，使用单个RGB相机，Chronos在四个任务上达到78%平均成功率，包括三个依赖记忆任务上的72%，而pi0.5总体为7%，依赖记忆子集为0%。这些结果表明，历史不应被视为辅助上下文，而应作为操控策略的潜在状态。
tldr_zh: Chronos将观测历史作为策略潜在状态，在记忆依赖任务上大幅超越马尔可夫基线。
cover_zh: 机器人记不住之前做了什么？这个框架用全历史状态让成功率飙升6倍！

score_breakdown.label: 关键词命中密集
score_breakdown.hint: 频道关键词命中 3 个（long-horizon manipulation、manipulation、dual-arm）

judge.reason: 提出非马尔可夫长程操作框架，结合物理信息与状态空间模型，显著超越VLA基线，具有高科研价值。

institutions: Huazhong University of Science and Technology
